title: BBC profiles Yann LeCun’s push beyond LLMs toward world models
friendlyTitle: Some AI researchers want tools that understand the real world better
summary: BBC News profiled Yann LeCun’s argument that current large language models remain limited for real-world intelligence and robotics. His new AMI Labs is pursuing JEPA-style world models that aim to reason about physical reality, causality, and action outcomes more flexibly.
friendlySummary: Yann LeCun says today’s chatbots are useful but not enough for robots and real-world reasoning. His new lab is working on a different kind of AI system.
whyItMatters: The debate matters because many daily-life AI breakthroughs, from home robots to autonomous assistants, require more than fluent text. If world-model approaches mature, the next major leap may come from grounded planning rather than simply bigger chatbots.
friendlyWhyItMatters: This could shape future AI that helps with physical tasks, not only writing, coding, and answering questions.
